ref: When the Midianite merchants came by, Joseph's brothers took him out of the well, and for
text: Nna Midianfo eguadzifo bi rutwa mu; na woyii Josef fii mona no mu, na wɔtɔɔn no maa Ismaelfo no gyee ndwetɛbona eduonu. Na wɔdze Josef kɔr Egypt.
translated: Then Midianites sold him into slavery; and they brought Joseph into Egypt, and they gave him to Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh.

ref: “Sir, the whole field, including the cave, is yours. With my own people as witnesses, I freely give it to you as a burial place for your dead.”
text: Dabida, mo wura, tsie me: haban no medze ma wo, na ɔbo tu a ɔwɔ mu no medze ma wo; wɔ me man mba hɔn enyim medze ma wo: sie wo fun.
translated: I will give you my wealth, I will give you my life, I will give you my all; I will give you my all; take my hand.

ref: He was sorry that he had made them,
text: EWURADZE nuu noho dɛ ɔyɛɛ nyimpa wɔ asaase do, na ɔyɛɛ no yaw wɔ n’akoma mu.
translated: He created man on earth, and he put life in him.

ref: So the king told his men to let Abram and Sarai take their possessions and leave.
text: Na Farao hyɛɛ nkorɔfo ma wokegyaa ɔno, nye ne yer, nye dza ɔwɔ nyinara wɔ ɔkwan do.
translated: The Pharaoh gave the people a feast, and he gave them his own food and the food of all his people.

ref: Jacob, also known as Israel, traveled to the south of Eder Tower, where he set up camp.
text: Na Israel tui, na ɔkɔbɔɔ n’asesee wɔ Eder abantsentsen ekyir noho.
translated: In Israel's land, he encamped his tents at the edge of Eder.
